Glossary

The Problem

You already have WPML.
Now what?

WPML gives you the structure. But translating the content is still on you — and every option comes with a catch.

Per-word pricing adds up fast

WPML's own translation services charge around €0.07 per word. A 10,000-word site costs €700 — and that's before any updates.

WPML Translation Services: ~€0.07/word

Manual translation doesn't scale

Every time you update a page, a product description or a blog post — someone has to translate it again. That's not a workflow, that's a bottleneck.

Typical agency rate: €0.10–0.20/word

Generic AI tools have no control

Other AI plugins support one provider, offer no glossary, no prompt control, no cost transparency — and still require you to manually copy results back into WPML.

No glossary. No profiles. No history.

Features

Everything you need to translate at scale

Not just a wrapper around an AI API. LinguaAI is a complete translation workflow — built specifically for WPML.

AI & Providers

OpenAI and Anthropic — both.

Choose between GPT-5, GPT-4o and Claude Haiku, Sonnet, Opus. Switch providers in settings. No other WPML AI plugin offers both.

GPT-5 · GPT-4o · Claude Haiku · Sonnet · Opus
Architecture

Background processing. No timeouts.

Translations run via OpenAI's Responses API — asynchronous, non-blocking. You click Translate, close the tab, and the plugin handles the rest.

Async · WP Cron polling · No HTTP timeout
Terminology

Glossary — your terms, every time.

Define product names, brand terms and technical vocabulary in CSV or JSON. Global or per language pair. Injected automatically into every prompt.

CSV · JSON · Global + per language pair
Workflow

Translation Profiles.

Bundle model + prompt template + glossary into a reusable preset. "Quick Draft" for blogs, "Precise + Full" for product pages. One click, consistent results.

Model · Prompt · Glossary — as one preset
Cost Control

Full cost transparency — per job.

See input, output and cached token counts for every translation. Open a breakdown modal to see how much each component (glossary, prompt, content) cost.

You pay OpenAI/Anthropic directly. No markup.
Prompt Control

Your instructions. Your output.

Write a custom system prompt or pick from reusable Prompt Templates per content type. Adjust per job. No code needed — everything in the WordPress admin.

System Prompt · Templates · Per-job override

Also included

ACF field translation
Bricks Builder support
Elementor support
Taxonomy term translation
Translate Again (re-translate)
3-second cancel window
Translation history log
Diagnostics & health checks

How it works

Three steps. That's it.

01

Install & connect your API key

Upload the plugin to WordPress, activate it, and enter your OpenAI or Anthropic API key in LinguaAI Settings. No configuration beyond that.

PHP 7.4+ · WordPress 6.2+ · WPML required

02

Pick content, choose languages, click Translate

Go to LinguaAI Translations, select the pages or posts you want to translate, pick your target languages, and hit Translate. Optionally choose a Profile or enable Glossary.

Posts · Pages · CPTs · Terms · ACF · Page Builders

03

Done. LinguaAI handles the rest.

Translations run in the background. No waiting, no timeouts. Once complete, the translated content is written directly into WPML — ready to publish.

Background processing · Direct WPML integration · History log

Comparison

See how LinguaAI compares

A direct feature and cost comparison with the most common alternatives for WPML users.

Feature
LinguaAI Pro from €49/year
WPML Translation ~€0.07/word
DeepL for WPML Subscription
AI Provider OpenAI + Anthropic OpenAI (via WPML) DeepL only
Background processing
Custom prompt control
Glossary / Terminology Limited
Translation Profiles
Full cost transparency
ACF Integration
Page Builder support Bricks + Elementor
Billing model API costs only ~€0.07/word Subscription
License price €49–99/year Per word, no cap Subscription

Real numbers: Translating 10,000 words with WPML Translation Services costs ~€700. With LinguaAI Pro + GPT-4o Mini, the same job costs around €0.50–5 in API fees — plus €49/year for the plugin license.

Pricing

Simple, transparent pricing

One flat license fee. No per-word charges. Your AI API costs go directly to OpenAI or Anthropic — no markup.

Free

€0 forever

Get started with AI translation on WordPress. Available on WordPress.org.

Download Free
  • OpenAI support (GPT-4o Mini, GPT-4o)
  • Post & page translation
  • Basic translation history
  • Basic settings
  • Anthropic Claude
  • Latest OpenAI models (GPT-5, GPT-5.4)

  • Translation Profiles

Most Popular

Pro — Single Site

€49 per year

For developers and site owners who need full control over their WPML translation workflow.

Get Pro
  • Everything in Free
  • Anthropic Claude (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus)
  • All OpenAI models incl. GPT-5, GPT-5.4
  • Taxonomy term translation
  • ACF field translation
  • Bricks Builder + Elementor support
  • Glossary (global + per language pair)
  • Prompt Templates + Translation Profiles
  • Cost tracking & breakdown per job
  • Diagnostics & priority support

Pro — Agency

€99 per year

For agencies and developers managing multiple client sites. One license, unlimited installs.

Get Agency
  • Everything in Pro Single Site
  • Unlimited WordPress installations
  • Same license key, unlimited sites
  • Priority support
  • Model compatibility updates

All prices in EUR. Licenses renew annually. Cancel anytime. API costs (OpenAI / Anthropic) are billed directly by the respective provider — not by LinguaAI.

FAQ

Common questions

Yes. LinguaAI is an add-on to WPML — it uses WPML's multilingual structure to store and manage translations. WPML Multilingual CMS is required.

LinguaAI supports OpenAI (GPT-4o, GPT-5, GPT-5.4 and more) and Anthropic Claude (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus). You can switch between providers in the settings at any time.

You pay OpenAI or Anthropic directly through your own API key. LinguaAI does not charge per word or per translation — you only pay the plugin license (Free or Pro) plus your direct API usage.

Costs depend on the AI model and content length. As a rough estimate: translating a 1,000-word page with GPT-4o Mini costs around €0.01–0.05. The plugin shows you the exact token count and cost for every job in the History view.

OpenAI automatically caches repeated prompt prefixes (your system prompt, glossary, website context). When the same prefix is used again, those tokens are served at a significantly lower rate — up to 90% cheaper. LinguaAI tracks cached tokens separately so you can see the savings.

Yes. You can write a custom system prompt, create reusable Prompt Templates for different content types, and use Translation Profiles to bundle model + prompt + glossary settings.

Use the Glossary feature. Define source and target terms in CSV or JSON format — globally or per language pair. The glossary is automatically injected into every translation prompt.

For OpenAI: no — and that's intentional. Translations are sent to OpenAI's background processing queue and retrieved by a WordPress cron job once complete. This prevents timeouts on long texts. For Anthropic: translations are synchronous (the Anthropic API works this way by design).

Yes. ACF fields are translated automatically (with per-field opt-out). Bricks Builder and Elementor content is extracted and re-injected correctly.

Yes — the content to be translated is sent to the respective API for processing. This is standard for AI-powered tools. Review OpenAI's and Anthropic's privacy policies for details on data handling. LinguaAI itself does not receive or store your content.

Get Started

Start translating today.

Free to start. Pro from €49/year. You own your API keys — no lock-in, no per-word fees.
